resolution: Declared nvidia-ml-py in requirements, pyproject, bootstrap core/compute requirements, and CPU validation lock.
Telemetry exceptions no longer clear valid CUDA snapshots; CUDA compute remains available when NVML telemetry is absent.
nvidia-smi rows are matched by UUID/PCI identity with conservative fallbacks rather than runtime row index.
Primary, persistent CUDA, persistent XPU sidecar, and one-shot XPU workers use one canonical device-binding function.
Sidecar reports total memory/hardware identity and performs FP64 scientific smoke before evaluator capability is accepted.
Completed runs record planned-vs-actual runtime/evaluator/optimizer/policy device attestation.
OS adapter labels no longer imply Task Manager numbering equivalence; stable hardware/runtime identity is authoritative.
